config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society’s interests matter a lot, but they should not automatically come before individual rights, because that is how abuse of power starts. A society is healthier when it protects basic rights even for unpopular or inconvenient people, while still allowing reasonable limits for safety and order. From my perspective, the state should serve people, not treat individuals as disposable for the sake of the majority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, pengalaman politik kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika negara diberi terlalu banyak kuasa atas nama “kepentingan bersama,” hak minoritas dan orang yang tidak populer sering jadi korban. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang mengorbankan individu demi mayoritas, melainkan yang bisa menjaga keduanya tanpa menjadikan hak sebagai hal yang bisa dinegosiasikan kapan saja.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically come before individual rights, because that is a dangerous path to abuse. From an Indonesian perspective, we know how easily “public interest” can be used to justify silencing dissent, punishing minorities, or protecting the powerful, so I am very cautious about giving society priority as a rule. Social order and shared obligations matter, but they should be limited by basic rights that cannot be overridden whenever the majority feels impatient or offended. A healthy society is not one that absorbs individuals into the collective, but one that protects people even when their rights are inconvenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu sebagai aturan umum. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa saat “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai tanpa batas, hasilnya sering justru penindasan terhadap minoritas, pembungkaman kritik, dan perluasan kuasa negara yang berbahaya. Saya paham argumen bahwa masyarakat butuh keteraturan dan tanggung jawab bersama, tetapi itu tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan perlindungan dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang kuat bukan yang mengorbankan individu demi mayoritas, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak bahkan ketika hal itu tidak nyaman bagi banyak orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests above individual rights as a general rule. From my Indonesian perspective, I have seen how easily “public interest” can be used to pressure dissenters, excuse unequal treatment, or protect those already in power, so I am very wary of giving the collective automatic priority. Society does matter, but its interests should be limited by non-negotiable rights, because once rights become flexible, the people who suffer first are usually the weak and the unpopular. A good society is not one that asks individuals to disappear for the majority, but one that can pursue common goals without sacrificing basic dignity and freedom.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai aturan umum, karena menurut saya kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika negara diberi alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” tanpa batas yang jelas, yang sering terjadi justru penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, terutama terhadap minoritas dan orang yang tidak populer. Argumen tentang keteraturan dan tanggung jawab bersama memang penting, tetapi itu harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang tidak mudah dinegosiasikan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang benar-benar kuat adalah masyarakat yang bisa mengejar tujuan bersama tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
